title: Concurrency, Shared State, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Load Shedding · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective concurrency budget and overload decision model; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in concurrency budget and overload decision model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat handlers as concurrent, protect shared state with ownership or synchronization, and verify with race tests under changed schedules.
Bound active expensive work with semaphores, worker pools, queue capacity, context-aware admission, fairness, and per-tenant partitions.
Choose trusted identity, token bucket or window semantics, burst, refill, distributed consistency, Retry-After, and bypass policy from abuse risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Inspect the failure record · Debug Handler Concurrency Race
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 1256 in a internal administrative API crossing two trust zones. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine concurrency capacity with a separate tenant rate budget; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat handlers as concurrent, protect shared state with ownership or synchronization, and verify with race tests under changed schedules.
